title: epidendrum harrisoniae
intro: Epidendrum harrisoniae, also known as the Harrison's orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Epidendrum harrisoniae include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and soothe sore throats. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions and decoctions, which can be made by steeping the roots or leaves in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
